message:
perf(bulkhead,dns,shutdown): replace Mutex-guarded metrics and timestamps with lock-free AtomicU64

Three subsystems held simple counters or timestamps behind Mutex/RwLock where
atomic operations suffice:

- Bulkhead: the RwLock<BulkheadMetrics> struct is decomposed into 6 individual
  AtomicU64 fields (total_executed, total_queued, total_rejected, total_timeout,
  total_cancelled, max_queue_wait_ms). The metrics() method reconstructs the
  snapshot from atomics. max_queue_wait_ms uses fetch_max for lock-free
  monotonic tracking. The on_full callback now receives a fresh metrics()
  snapshot after dropping the queue lock, avoiding holding two locks.

- DnsCache: the RwLock<CacheStats> is replaced with three AtomicU64 fields
  (stat_hits, stat_misses, stat_evictions). The stats() method builds the
  CacheStats struct from atomic loads plus cache.len(). clear() resets all
  three atomics to 0.

- ShutdownSignal: drain_deadline and drain_start were Mutex<Option<Time>>.
  Now AtomicU64 with 0 = None convention (valid Time values are always >0
  in practice). Reads use Acquire ordering; writes use Release for
  happens-before visibility to phase observers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
